# `TCP S->C 0x8383` — InfoServer ServerList

**Transport:** TCP  
**Direction:** S->C  
**Identifier:** `0x8383`  
**Status:** verified

## Structure

The InfoServer's reply to `0x8482 GetCharList` (which on the
InfoServer phase doubles as a "GetServerList" request — the server
returns the list of game servers the account can play on, not
characters). The 26-byte body is a count-prefixed array of
fixed-shape server records each followed by the server name string.

```
Offset  Size  Field             Value (retail "titan")
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 0      2     opcode             83 83
 2      2     server_count LE16  01 00          (always 1 in retail)
 4      2     record_size LE16   0e 00          (14B per fixed-record)

  ── record #0 (14B fixed + variable name) ────────────────────
 6      4     server_ip   bytes  9d 5a c3 4a    = 157.90.195.74
10      4     udp_port    LE32   e0 2e 00 00    = 12000
14      1     name_len_terminated     06        = strlen(name) + 1
15      1     chars_per_account       04
16      2     online_users LE16  XX 00          variable (see below)
18      2     unknown_flag LE16  YY 00          variable (see below)
20      N     name (C-string)    74 69 74 61 6e 00   "titan\0"
                                  (length = name_len_terminated)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
total  26 bytes (when server_count=1, name_len_terminated=6)
```

### Field semantics (verified vs inferred)

* **server_ip** — `9d 5a c3 4a` decodes to `157.90.195.74`, which
  is the IP every retail capture in the corpus connects to (verified
  by `find_server_ip()` in `tools/pcap-decode.py` over each pcap;
  399 packets in `RETAIL_DRSTONE_20260501_172522` resolve to that
  peer). The bytes are written network-order — i.e. each octet at
  its in-address position — so the protocol is "raw IPv4 address
  bytes" rather than a numeric LE encoding. **Verified.**

* **udp_port** — `e0 2e 00 00` (LE32) = 12000, the canonical NC2
  GameServer UDP port. **Verified.**

* **name_len_terminated** — single byte = `strlen(name) + 1` (i.e.
  including the trailing NUL). For "titan" this is 6. **Verified
  against the trailing string length in every sample.**

* **chars_per_account** — single byte. All 25 retail samples carry
  `0x04`, matching NC2 Evolution's documented limit of 4 characters
  per account.

* **online_users LE16** — varies across captures from `0x0003` to
  `0x0019` (3 to 25). The value tracks the wall-clock time of each
  capture in a way that's consistent with **online player count /
  load**:

  | capture                                | local time    | value |
  |----------------------------------------|---------------|------:|
  | ZONING_AND_ITEMS_LONG_20260502_010613  | 02/05 01:06   |     3 |
  | AUGUSTO_20260426_201952                | 26/04 20:19   |     6 |
  | HANNIBAL_20260426_201501               | 26/04 20:15   |     6 |
  | ODA_20260426_202428                    | 26/04 20:24   |     6 |
  | NORMAN_20260426_200458                 | 26/04 20:04   |     7 |
  | DRSTONE_20260501_175315                | 01/05 17:53   |     7 |
  | DRSTONE4_20260501_193336               | 01/05 19:33   |     8 |
  | DRSTONE_20260501_172522                | 01/05 17:25   |     9 |
  | DRSTONE3_20260501_181349               | 01/05 18:13   |    12 |
  | CREATION_LEVELING_LONG (t=2652)        | 02/05 16:53   |    15 |
  | CREATION_LEVELING_LONG (t=1.7)         | 02/05 16:08   |    17 |
  | RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE (t=1396)          | 03/05 14:39   |    17 |
  | RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE (t=1319)          | 03/05 14:38   |    18 |
  | FULL_PCAP_TRACE_20260426_154648        | 26/04 15:46   |    19 |
  | CASH_VENDOR_PCAP_FRESH_20260426_143918 | 26/04 14:39   |    25 |

  The upper byte is always zero so an LE16 player-count is the
  natural read; the value never exceeds 25 (well below the actual
  retail population) so it could equivalently be a percentage.
  **Inferred.**

* **unknown_flag LE16** — `0x0006` on every 26/04 capture and
  `0x0007` on every May capture. The transition lies between the
  26/04 20:24 (ODA) and 01/05 17:25 (DRSTONE) captures, which
  brackets a server-side patch window. Likely a server config
  revision / patch generation counter. **Inferred.**

### Same packet at non-login timestamps

In `CREATION_LEVELING_LONG`, `RETAIL_VEHICLE_DRONE`, and
`ZONING_AND_ITEMS_LONG` the server emits an extra `0x8383` deep
into the session (t=2652s, t=1319s/1396s, t=21s respectively).
These all follow the user backing out to the character-select
screen, which re-runs the InfoServer phase on a fresh TCP
connection — i.e. the packet itself isn't a runtime channel; the
captures simply contain multiple login flows.

## Variants

Only one wire variant has been observed: a single-server reply
where `server_count=1`, `record_size=14`, and the trailing name is
"titan\0". The packet structure trivially supports multi-server
replies (loop the 14B fixed record + variable name `server_count`
times) but no such reply exists in any retail capture, so the
multi-server layout is unverified.

## Observed contexts

* **InfoServer Phase 1 (login)** — the reply to a client
  `0x8482 GetCharList` on the TCP :7000 (InfoServer) connection.
  Documented in `docs/protocol/flows/login.md` lines 51-52
  (sequence diagram step 7).

* **Re-entry to character-select during a session** — when the user
  exits to the character-select screen mid-game, the client
  reconnects to the InfoServer and the same exchange repeats. This
  is why `0x8383` shows up at t≫1.8s in the long-running captures.

## Open questions

* **online_users units**: the LE16 reads in [3, 25] and is far
  below any plausible NC2 population, so it could be a percentage,
  bucketed load (0..100 mapped to 0..25), or a raw player count
  for whatever subset of the playerbase the InfoServer is
  reporting. Need either retail server-side docs or a stress-test
  capture to pin the unit.

* **unknown_flag (LE16 0x0006/0x0007)**: server config revision is
  the leading hypothesis based on the 26/04→01/05 jump. A second
  pre-26/04 capture would falsify or confirm the
  "monotonically increasing" reading.

* **multi-server reply**: the layout is mechanically derivable
  but unverified. If/when retail ever ships a second game server,
  recapture and confirm the tail walks correctly.

## Server-side handler

`server.infoserver.packets.server_tcp.ServerList` already builds
this packet. The byte layout in the existing implementation matches
retail — the only differences are the placeholder values for
`online_users` (currently `99`, retail uses live load) and
`unknown_flag` (currently `127`, retail uses `6` or `7`). No
behavioural change is needed for client compatibility; the values
are documented here for fidelity work and pinned by
`server.infoserver.packets.server_tcp.ServerListByteIdentityTest`.
